language.description: Set the language for reviews by using the corresponding ISO language code.

tone_instructions.description: Set the tone of reviews and chat. Example: 'You must talk like Mr. T. I pity the fool who doesn't!'

enable_free_tier.description: Enable free tier features for users not on a paid plan.

inheritance.description: Inheritance | Use parent settings for any values not defined here. Inheritance only propagates upward if enabled at each level.

profile.description: Set the review profile: quiet for only the most important feedback, chill for balanced feedback, assertive for more feedback (which may feel nitpicky).

request_changes_workflow.description: Automatically approve once CodeRabbit’s comments are resolved and no pre-merge checks are failing. Note: In GitLab, all discussions must be resolved.

high_level_summary.description: Generate a high-level summary of the changes in the PR description or walkthrough.

high_level_summary_instructions.description: By default, CodeRabbit generates release notes in the description. Use this to customize the summary content and format. Example: 'Create concise release notes as a bullet-point list, followed by a Markdown table showing lines added and removed by each contributing author.' Note: Use `high_level_summary_in_walkthrough` to place the summary in the walkthrough instead of the description.

high_level_summary_placeholder.description: Placeholder in the PR description that CodeRabbit replaces with the high-level summary. If `high_level_summary` is false, the summary is still generated when this placeholder is present.

high_level_summary_in_walkthrough.description: Include the high-level summary in the walkthrough comment.

auto_title_placeholder.description: Add this keyword to the PR title to auto-generate a title.

auto_title_instructions.description: Auto Title Instructions | Customize how CodeRabbit generates the PR title.

review_status.description: Post review status messages (e.g., when a review is skipped) in the walkthrough summary comment.

review_details.description: Post review details (ignored files, extra context used, suppressed comments, etc.).

review_progress.description: Publish the canonical user-facing review status and progress updates (GitHub progress reports/check runs today; other platform equivalents may follow).

commit_status.description: Mirror review progress using legacy commit statuses for compatibility with required checks and existing automations. This setting is only used when review_progress is disabled.

fail_commit_status.description: On review errors, fail the active outward review status surface. When review_progress is enabled this applies to progress reporting; otherwise it applies to the legacy commit-status mirror when enabled.

collapse_walkthrough.description: Wrap the walkthrough in a Markdown collapsible section.

changed_files_summary.description: Include a summary of the changed files in the walkthrough.

sequence_diagrams.description: Include sequence diagrams in the walkthrough.

estimate_code_review_effort.description: Include an estimated code review effort in the walkthrough.

assess_linked_issues.description: Include an assessment of how well the changes address linked issues in the walkthrough.

related_issues.description: Include potentially related issues in the walkthrough.

related_prs.description: Related PRs | Include potentially related PRs in the walkthrough.

suggested_labels.description: Suggest labels based on the changes, and include them in the walkthrough.

labeling_instructions.description: Labeling Instructions | Define allowed labels and when to suggest them. When provided, CodeRabbit suggests only from this list (still informed by prior PRs); when empty, suggestions rely entirely on prior PRs.

label.description: Label to suggest for the PR. Example: frontend

instructions.description: Instructions for the label. Example: Apply when the PR contains changes to React components.

mutually_exclusive_groups.description: Mutually Exclusive Groups | Define labels that should not coexist. Example: `{ risk: ['critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low'] }`.

auto_apply_labels.description: Automatically apply suggested labels to the PR.

suggested_reviewers.description: Suggest reviewers based on the changes, and include them in the walkthrough.

auto_assign_reviewers.description: Automatically assign the suggested reviewers to the PR.

suggested_reviewers_instructions.description: Reviewer Instructions | Map reviewers (users or team) to PR scenarios where they should be assigned. When empty, suggestions rely on prior PRs. Team handles are supported only on GitHub.

handle.description: Reviewer username or team slug to assign. Example: security-pdl

type.description: Whether the reviewer is an individual user or a team/group.

reviewers.description: List of reviewers to assign when the PR matches the instructions below. Each entry pairs a handle (username or team slug) with its type (user or group).

instructions.description: Instructions for when to assign these reviewers. Example: Assign when the PR contains Windows registry changes.

in_progress_fortune.description: Post a fortune message while the review is running.

poem.description: Generate a poem in the walkthrough comment.

enable_prompt_for_ai_agents.description: Prompt for AI Agents | Include the '🤖 Prompt for AI Agents' section in inline review comments to provide codegen instructions for AI agents.

path_filters.description: Specify file patterns to include or exclude in a review using glob patterns (e.g., `!dist/**`, `src/**`). These patterns also apply to 'git sparse-checkout', including specified patterns and ignoring excluded ones (starting with '!') when cloning the repository.

path_instructions.description: Path Instructions | Add path-specific guidance for code review.

instructions.description: Additional review guidance for matching paths.

abort_on_close.description: Abort the in-progress review if the PR is closed or merged.

disable_cache.description: Disable caching of code and dependencies; fetch them fresh on each run.

slop_detection.description: Anti-Slop | Settings for detecting and managing spam or low-quality PRs. Only for public repos, only for GitHub as of now.

enabled.description: Enable or disable slop and low-quality PR detection.

label.description: Label to apply to the PR when it is classified as slop. Example: slop

enabled.description: Automatic Review | Review PRs automatically.

description_keyword.description: Keyword in the PR description that triggers a review when automatic reviews are disabled. If `enabled` is false and this field is not empty, CodeRabbit reviews the PR only when this keyword is present in the description.

auto_incremental_review.description: Incremental Review | Re-run the review on each push.

auto_pause_after_reviewed_commits.description: Auto Pause After Reviewed Commits | Pause automatic reviews after this many reviewed commits since the last pause. Set to 0 to disable.

ignore_title_keywords.description: Skip reviews when the PR title contains any of these keywords (case-insensitive).

labels.description: Labels that control which PRs are reviewed. Labels starting with '!' are negative matches. Examples: ['bug', 'feature'] reviews PRs with either label. ['!wip'] reviews all PRs except those labeled 'wip' when automatic reviews are enabled. ['bug', '!wip'] reviews PRs labeled 'bug' but not 'wip'. When `enabled` is false, a positive label match (for example ['review-ready']) triggers a review; negative-only labels such as ['!wip'] remain exclusion filters and do not opt PRs in by themselves.

base_branches.description: Base branches (other than the default branch) to review. Accepts regex patterns. Use '.*' to match all branches.

ignore_usernames.description: Skip reviews for PRs authored by these usernames (exact match; not email addresses).

enabled.description: Docstrings | Enable the docstrings finishing touch (trigger via the 📝 Generate docstrings checkbox or `@coderabbitai generate docstrings`). CodeRabbit generates or improves docstrings for functions changed in the PR and opens a follow-up PR containing the docstring edits.

unit_tests.description: Unit Tests | Configure unit test generation.

enabled.description: Unit Tests | Generate unit tests for changes in PRs.
Simplify | Enable the simplify finishing touch (trigger via the ✨ Simplify code checkbox). CodeRabbit reviews changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then applies targeted improvements.

Autofix | Enable the autofix finishing touch (trigger via the 🪄 Autofix checkboxes under review comments or the `@coderabbitai autofix` command). When disabled, the autofix checkboxes are hidden, the command is removed from help, and CodeRabbit declines `@coderabbitai autofix` requests.

custom.description: Custom Recipes | Define up to 5 custom finishing touch recipes. Trigger a recipe with `@coderabbitai run <recipe name>`.

enabled.description: Enabled | Enable this custom finishing touch recipe.

name.description: Name | Recipe name used in commands (for example: `@coderabbitai run cleanup stale imports`). Run with `@coderabbitai run <recipe name>`.

instructions.description: Instructions | Describe what this recipe should do. Trigger with `@coderabbitai run <recipe name>` or the checkbox under "✨ Finishing Touches". CodeRabbit provides PR context and runs this with an agent.

override_requested_reviewers_only.description: Override Requested Reviewers Only | When enabled, only requested reviewers (and not the pull request author) can override/ignore failing pre-merge checks.

docstrings.description: Docstring Coverage | Check that docstring coverage meets the configured threshold.

mode.description: Mode | Enforcement level: `off` disables the check, `warning` posts a warning, and `error` requires resolution before merging. If the request-changes workflow is enabled, `error` can block the PR until the check passes.

threshold.description: Threshold | Minimum docstring coverage (%) required to pass.

title.description: Title Check | Validate the PR title against the requirements.

mode.description: Mode | Enforcement level: `off` disables the check, `warning` posts a warning, and `error` requires resolution before merging. If the request-changes workflow is enabled, `error` can block the PR until the check passes.

requirements.description: Requirements | Describe title requirements. Example: 'Title should be concise and descriptive, ideally under 50 characters.'

description.description: Description Check | Check that the PR description follows best practices.

mode.description: Mode | Enforcement level: `off` disables the check, `warning` posts a warning, and `error` requires resolution before merging. If the request-changes workflow is enabled, `error` can block the PR until the check passes.

issue_assessment.description: Linked Issue Assessment | Assess how well the PR addresses linked issues.

mode.description: Mode | Enforcement level: `off` disables the check, `warning` posts a warning, and `error` requires resolution before merging. If the request-changes workflow is enabled, `error` can block the PR until the check passes.

custom_checks.description: Custom Pre-merge Checks | Define custom checks that must pass before merging. Each check needs a unique name (≤50 chars) and deterministic instructions (≤10,000 chars).

mode.description: Mode | Enforcement level: `off` disables the check, `warning` posts a warning, and `error` requires resolution before merging. If the request-changes workflow is enabled, `error` can block the PR until the check passes.

post_merge_actions.description: Custom Post-merge Actions | Define custom actions to run after merging. Each action needs a unique name (≤100 chars) and a deterministic prompt (≤10,000 chars).

enabled.description: Enabled | Run this action after a PR is merged.

prompt.description: Prompt | Deterministic instructions describing the action to perform (max 10,000 characters).

tools.description: Tools that provide additional context to code reviews.

ast-grep.description: Enable ast-grep | ast-grep is a code analysis tool that helps you to find patterns in your codebase using abstract syntax trees patterns. | v0.44.1

shellcheck.description: ShellCheck is a static analysis tool that finds bugs in your shell scripts.

enabled.description: Enable ShellCheck | ShellCheck is a static analysis tool that finds bugs in your shell. | Enable ShellCheck integration. | v0.11.0

ruff.description: Ruff is a Python linter and code formatter.

enabled.description: Enable Ruff | Ruff is a Python linter and code formatter. |  Enable Ruff integration. | v0.15.21

markdownlint.description: markdownlint-cli2 is a static analysis tool to enforce standards and consistency for Markdown files.

enabled.description: Enable markdownlint | markdownlint-cli2 is a static analysis tool to enforce standards and consistency for Markdown files. | Enable markdownlint integration. | v0.23.0

Enable GitHub Checks
			| Enable integration, defaults to true
			| Enable GitHub Checks integration.

timeout_ms.description: Time in milliseconds to wait for all GitHub Checks to conclude. Default 90 seconds, max 15 minutes (900000ms).

languagetool.description: LanguageTool is a style and grammar checker for 30+ languages.

enabled.description: Enable LanguageTool | Enable LanguageTool integration.

enabled_rules.description: IDs of rules to be enabled. The rule won't run unless 'level' is set to a level that activates the rule.

disabled_rules.description: IDs of rules to be disabled. Note: EN_UNPAIRED_BRACKETS, and EN_UNPAIRED_QUOTES are always disabled.

enabled_categories.description: IDs of categories to be enabled.

disabled_categories.description: IDs of categories to be disabled. Note: TYPOS, TYPOGRAPHY, and CASING are always disabled.

enabled_only.description: Only the rules and categories whose IDs are specified with 'enabledRules' or 'enabledCategories' are enabled.

level.description: If set to 'picky', additional rules will be activated, i.e. rules that you might only find useful when checking formal text.

biome.description: Biome is a fast formatter, linter, and analyzer for web projects.

enabled.description: Enable Biome | Biome is a fast formatter, linter, and analyzer for web projects. | Enable Biome integration. | v2.5.3
Enable Hadolint | Hadolint is a Dockerfile linter. | Enable Hadolint integration. | v2.14.0
Enable SwiftLint | SwiftLint is a Swift linter. | Enable SwiftLint integration. | v0.65.0

config_file.description: Optional path to the SwiftLint configuration file relative to the repository. This is useful when the configuration file is named differently than the default '.swiftlint.yml' or '.swiftlint.yaml'.

phpstan.description: PHPStan is a tool to analyze PHP code.

enabled.description: Enable PHPStan | PHPStan requires [config file](https://phpstan.org/config-reference#config-file) in your repository root. Please ensure that this file contains the `paths:` parameter. | v2.2.5

level.description: Level | Specify the [rule level](https://phpstan.org/user-guide/rule-levels) to run. When set to `default`, the level is determined by the review profile: `chill` uses level 3 (real bugs only — return/property type mismatches, array offset errors) and `assertive` uses level 8 (adds dead code detection, argument type checking, null safety, and typehint checks). This setting is ignored if your configuration file already has a `level:` parameter.

phpmd.description: PHPMD is a tool to find potential problems in PHP code.

enabled.description: Enable PHPMD | PHPMD is a tool to find potential problems in PHP code. | v2.15.0

phpcs.description: PHP CodeSniffer is a PHP linter and coding standard checker.

enabled.description: Enable PHP CodeSniffer | PHP CodeSniffer is a PHP linter and coding standard checker. | v3.7.2

golangci-lint.description: golangci-lint is a fast linters runner for Go.

enabled.description: Enable golangci-lint | golangci-lint is a fast linters runner for Go. | Enable golangci-lint integration. | v2.12.2

config_file.description: Optional path to the golangci-lint configuration file relative to the repository. Useful when the configuration file is named differently than the default '.golangci.yml', '.golangci.yaml', '.golangci.toml', '.golangci.json'.

yamllint.description: YAMLlint is a linter for YAML files.

enabled.description: Enable YAMLlint | YAMLlint is a linter for YAML files. | Enable YAMLlint integration. | v1.37.1

gitleaks.description: Betterleaks is a secret scanner (an improved version of Gitleaks).

enabled.description: Enable Betterleaks | Betterleaks is a secret scanner (an improved version of Gitleaks). | Enable Betterleaks integration. | v1.6.1

trufflehog.description: TruffleHog is a secret scanner with verification capabilities that can detect and verify secrets in code.

enabled.description: Enable TruffleHog | TruffleHog is a secret scanner with verification capabilities. | Enable TruffleHog integration. | v3.95.9

checkov.description: Checkov is a static code analysis tool for infrastructure-as-code files.

enabled.description: Enable Checkov | Checkov is a static code analysis tool for infrastructure-as-code files. | v3.3.8

tflint.description: TFLint is a Terraform linter for finding potential errors and enforcing best practices.

enabled.description: Enable TFLint | TFLint is a Terraform linter for finding potential errors. | v0.63.1

detekt.description: Detekt is a static code analysis tool for Kotlin files.

config_file.description: Optional path to the detekt configuration file relative to the repository.

e18e.description: @e18e/eslint-plugin modernization, performance, and dependency replacement checks.

enabled.description: Enable Flake8 | Flake8 is a Python linter that wraps PyFlakes, pycodestyle and Ned Batchelder's McCabe script. | v7.3.0

fbinfer.description: Configuration for Infer to find bugs in Java and C/C++ code

enabled.description: Enable Infer for static bug analysis in Java and C/C++ code | v1.3.0

enable_java.description: Enable Java analysis | Disabled by default because Java analysis may require compiling more than the changed files.

fortitudeLint.description: Fortitude is a Fortran linter that checks for code quality and style issues.

enabled.description: Enable Fortitude | Fortitude is a Fortran linter that checks for code quality and style issues | v0.9.0

buf.description: Buf offers linting for Protobuf files.

config_file.description: Optional path to the PMD configuration file relative to the repository.

cppcheck.description: Cppcheck is a static code analysis tool for the C and C++ programming languages.

config_file.description: Optional path to the Semgrep configuration file relative to the repository.

circleci.description: CircleCI tool is a static checker for CircleCI config files.

Enable HTMLHint | HTMLHint is a static code analysis tool for HTML files. | Enable HTMLHint integration. | v1.9.2

art.description: Generate art in chat responses (ASCII or emoji).

allow_non_org_members.description: Allow non-organization members to interact with CodeRabbit in comment chat. Set to false to restrict issue/review comment interactions to organization members on GitHub organization repositories. This does not affect automatic PR review eligibility.

auto_reply.description: Let CodeRabbit reply automatically without requiring a mention/tag.

usage.description: Jira | Allow creating Jira issues from chat. 'auto' disables the integration for public repositories.
Linear | Allow creating Linear issues from chat. 'auto' disables the integration for public repositories.

opt_out.description: Opt Out | Disable knowledge base features that require data retention. Opting out removes any existing stored knowledge base data.

enabled.description: Web Search | Use web search to gather additional context.

code_guidelines.description: Use your coding guideline documents (see File Patterns) as review criteria.

enabled.description: Enabled | Apply your organization's coding standards during reviews.

filePatterns.description: File Patterns | Patterns for your coding guideline documents. CodeRabbit scans these files to learn your standards and apply them during reviews. Multiple files supported; file names are case-sensitive. Defaults include: (**/.cursorrules, .github/copilot-instructions.md, **/CLAUDE.md, **/GEMINI.md, **/.cursor/rules/*, **/.windsurfrules, **/.clinerules/*, **/.rules/*, **/AGENT.md, **/AGENTS.md, **/REVIEW.md).

files.description: Files | Comma-separated glob patterns for guideline files (e.g., `**/AGENTS.md`). Multiple patterns supported. File names are case-sensitive.

applyTo.description: Apply To | Comma-separated glob patterns for files these guidelines applies to (e.g., `**/*.ts`). Multiple patterns supported. File names are case-sensitive.

scope.description: Learnings | Choose scope: 'local' (repo), 'global' (org), or 'auto' (local for public repos, global for private repos).

approval_delay.description: Days admins have to approve or reject a learning before it is automatically applied. Must be an integer between 0 and 30. Set to 0 to apply learnings immediately without approval.

scope.description: Issues | Choose scope for GitHub/GitLab issues: 'local' (repo), 'global' (org), or 'auto' (local for public repos, global for private repos).

usage.description: Jira | Use Jira as a knowledge source. 'auto' disables the integration for public repositories.

project_keys.description: Jira Project Keys | Restrict Jira context to these projects.

usage.description: Linear | Use Linear as a knowledge source. 'auto' disables the integration for public repositories.

team_keys.description: Linear Team Keys | Restrict Linear context to these teams (e.g. 'ENG').

scope.description: PRs | Choose scope: 'local' (repo), 'global' (org), or 'auto' (local for public repos, global for private repos).

usage.description: MCP | Use MCP servers as a knowledge source. 'auto' disables the integration for public repositories.

disabled_servers.description: Disabled MCP Servers | Specify MCP server labels to disable (case-insensitive). These servers will be excluded from reviews and knowledge base queries.

automatic_repository_linking.description: Automatic Repository Linking | When enabled, CodeRabbit automatically detects and links related repositories in your organization to identify breaking changes and downstream impact during PR reviews.

linked_repositories.description: Linked Repositories | Repositories that CodeRabbit should consider when reviewing PRs in this repo. Use this to surface cross-repo dependencies and catch breaking changes across related codebases.

repository.description: Repository name in owner/repo format (e.g., GitHub: myorg/backend-api, Azure DevOps: MyProject/backend-api, GitLab: group/subgroup/repo, Bitbucket: workspace/repo)

instructions.description: Optionally provide description and guidance on what this repository contains for CodeRabbit to consider during reviews. Example: 'Contains REST API endpoints and database models.'

docstrings.description: Docstring Generation | Settings for generating docstrings.

language.description: Language for generated docstrings (ISO language code).

path_instructions.description: Path Instructions | Add path-specific guidelines for docstring generation.

instructions.description: Additional docstring-generation guidelines for matching paths.

unit_tests.description: Unit Test Generation | Settings for generating unit tests.

path_instructions.description: Path Instructions | Add path-specific guidelines for unit test generation.

instructions.description: Additional unit-test-generation guidelines for matching paths.

enabled.description: Automatic Issue Enrichment | Analyze and enrich issues with additional context (related code, potential solutions, complexity assessment).
Issue Planning | Generate an implementation plan for issues (early preview).
Automatic Planning | Trigger issue planning based on labels.

labels.description: Labels that trigger automatic issue planning. Labels starting with '!' are negative matches. Examples: ['feature', 'enhancement'] plans issues with either label. ['!wip'] plans all issues except those labeled 'wip'. ['feature', '!wip'] plans issues labeled 'feature' but not 'wip'.

labeling_instructions.description: Labeling Instructions | Define issue labels to suggest and when to suggest them.

label.description: Label to suggest for the issue. Example: enhancement

instructions.description: Instructions for the label. Example: New feature or request.

auto_apply_labels.description: Automatically apply suggested labels to the issue. When enabled without labeling instructions, labels are auto-suggested based on similar issues.
